mappings.rationale: Directly covers the history and techniques of surface, undersea, and space exploration.
Relevant to surface and underground exploration for determining Earth's interior properties and resource discovery.
Covers geophysical methods of exploration used for indirect resource discovery and determining Earth's interior properties.
Relevant to understanding Earth's interior structure and the geological processes underlying surface and underground exploration.
Directly relevant to undersea exploration including surface vessels, submersibles, and oceanographic measurement.

mappings.vsiAuthor: David George Bowers, Emyr Martyn Roberts
mappings.rationale: Relevant to oceanographic measurement techniques and navigational methods for undersea exploration.
Relevant to undersea exploration platforms and the biological dimensions of ocean exploration.
Relevant to undersea exploration techniques and oceanographic sampling methods.
Relevant to surface exploration of extreme environments and scientific exploration of Earth's properties.
Relevant to surface exploration techniques and life-support systems in extreme environments.
Directly relevant to unmanned spacecraft probing the planets and their satellites.
Relevant to space probes exploring planetary satellites, a key topic in the section's unmanned space programs.
Relevant to interplanetary trajectories and the scientific goals of space exploration.
Relevant to astronomical observations and the scientific contributions of space exploration.
Relevant to the scientific contributions of space exploration and astronomical observations.
Relevant to the scientific contributions of space exploration to advances in the physical sciences.
Relevant to space exploration observations of the Sun and the space environment affecting spacecraft.
Relevant to the astronomical observations enabled by space exploration and orbiting satellites.
Relevant to the search for life as a scientific goal of space and planetary exploration.
Relevant to sounding rockets exploring Earth's upper atmosphere and the environment of space affecting reentry.
Relevant to Earth-oriented satellites for communications, surveys, and the scientific exploration of Earth's systems.
Relevant to resource exploration for the discovery of ores, building materials, and fuels.
Relevant to direct exploration methods including boring and sampling of soil and rock.
Relevant to navigational methods for establishing precise locations during undersea and space exploration.
Relevant to astronomical observations and the space-based telescopes used in space exploration programs.
Relevant to surface exploration of geothermal energy sources and determining Earth's interior properties.
Relevant to surface exploration techniques and resource discovery in arid environments.
Relevant to surface and underground exploration techniques for scientific and resource purposes.
Relevant to surface exploration and the determination of Earth's properties from glacial evidence.
Covers early speculations and the development of space flight theory that preceded the space programs.
